final_response: Logged. Postgres for the events service is a tech-stack commitment that constrains future work, so it went into decisions.md as a 2026-05-17 entry (status accepted), with a pointer line in today's daybook log.
actions: Read AGENTS.md, decisions.md, observations.md, daybook/AGENTS.md to confirm routing rules.
Classified 'events service will use Postgres' as a decision (constrains future action — schema, clients, ops all target Postgres) rather than an observation (descriptive, non-binding).
Appended '## 2026-05-17 — Events service uses Postgres' to decisions.md with Status: accepted, Generated-by: mixed, and Context/Decision/Consequences/References fields.
Created daybook/2026-05-17.md with Intent/Log/Handoff sections and a Log entry pointing to the decision anchor.
Did not touch observations.md — the input is prescriptive, not descriptive.
notes: Routing call: 'we're going to use Postgres' is a forward-binding tech-stack choice. The decisions.md bar test ('would a future reader ask: are we allowed to use MySQL?') resolves yes, so this is a decision, not an observation.
Used Generated-by: mixed because the commitment is the user's but the entry text is agent-produced.
Used the canonical decision anchor format YYYY-MM-DD-short-title so daybook cross-reference resolves.
Did not write a Handoff section yet — this is mid-session, not end-of-day; AGENTS.md says handoff is written before EOD commit.
